areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The All-CNN-C model is implemented exactly as in Table 2 (Section 3.1): every max-pooling layer of base model C is replaced by a 3x3 convolu
leaves.why: models.py build_all_cnn (letter 'C', variant 'allcnn') replaces each maxpool with _conv_relu(96,96,3,stride=2,padding=1)/_conv_relu(192,192,3,stride=2,padding=1) (channels preserved) and ends with conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1) followed by GAP in AllCNNModel.fo

leaves.label: The three base models A, B, and C from Table 1 (Section 3.1) are implemented: model B as the Network-in-Network variant with one 1-by-1 conv
leaves.why: models.py implements letter A (single 5×5 conv per block + maxpool), letter B (5×5 conv followed by a 1×1 NiN conv per block + maxpool), and letter C (two 3×3 convs per block + maxpool), each topped by the conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1)+GAP head, matching Table

leaves.label: The Strided-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by removing max-pooling and increasing the stride of the convolution layer p
leaves.why: models.py variant 'strided' for letter C sets stride=2 on the conv immediately preceding each removed pool (_conv_relu(96,96,3,stride=2,padding=1) and _conv_relu(192,192,3,stride=2,padding=1)) and omits _maxpool(); the c_strided_cifar10_noaug output cell shows it was trained.

leaves.label: The ConvPool-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by placing a dense convolution layer, with the same kernel size as the resp
leaves.why: models.py variant 'convpool' inserts an extra dense 3×3 conv (_conv_relu(96,96,3,1,1) / _conv_relu(192,192,3,1,1) — same kernel size as the 3×3 pool) before each retained _maxpool(); output cells a/b/c_convpool_cifar10_noaug confirm execution.

leaves.label: Guided backpropagation is implemented as defined in Section 4 / Figure 1: when backpropagating through a ReLU, values are masked to zero whe
leaves.why: guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes guided_grad = grad * (input>0).float() * (grad>0).float(), exactly the combined deconvnet+backprop ReLU mask with no pooling switches, and outputs/c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/guided_backprop.json + fig_relu_masking.png show

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky & Hinton, 2009) images are whitened and contrast normalized following Goodfellow et al. (2013), as stated
leaves.why: data.py applies per-sample GCN (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening (compute_zca_matrix, epsilon=0.1, fit on train set) per Goodfellow et al., and cached matrices data/zca_cache/zca_cifar10_eps0.1.npz and zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz confirm it was applied to both datasets.

leaves.label: The data-augmentation runs use exactly the augmentations of Section 3.2: horizontally flipped copies of all images plus random translations
leaves.why: data.py _augment implements only hflip + random translation with pad=5 (max ±5px) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 up to the named online-flip/reflect-pad detail.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-100 experiment reuses the identical All-CNN-C model and identical data pipeline and hyperparameters (learning rate and its schedul
leaves.why: data.py load_cifar processes cifar100 through the identical GCN+ZCA pipeline (zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz cached), models.py instantiates the same AllCNNModel('C','allcnn') with num_classes=100, and only a single c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists (no per-dataset sweep dirs), but its

leaves.label: Training logs/configs show all CIFAR networks trained with stochastic gradient descent with fixed momentum 0.9 for a total of 350 epochs, wi
leaves.why: metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 and the measured train_loss_history exhibits the schedule's discrete drops (~0.67→0.558 at epoch ~200, ~0.52→0.498 at ~250, slight drop near 300, mirrored by test_acc_history jumping 0.79→0.834 after epoch 200), confirming the 35

leaves.label: Run artifacts show the learning rate γ was searched per model over exactly the fixed set {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01}, with results reported for
leaves.why: metrics.json carries lr/best_lr fields (best_lr=0.05 for a_base and a_strided) indicating a best-γ concept exists, but no measured results for the other three candidate rates appear in any visible artifact, so the searched-set requirement is only partially evidenced.

leaves.label: Configs/code apply dropout with probability 20% on the input image and 50% after each pooling layer (or after the layer replacing pooling),
leaves.why: models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every letter/variant, matching Section 3.2; only the weight-decay value is unverified.

leaves.label: The ImageNet run trains the upscaled All-CNN-B with 12 convolutional layers (conv1–conv12, Table 6 in the Appendix) for 450,000 iterations w
leaves.why: models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB concretely implements the 12-conv-layer Table 6 architecture (3+3+3+3 conv layers, fully-convolutional head), but the required training run is entirely absent.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 results are reported as test error (%) on the standard held-out test sets, separately for the no-augmentation and aug
leaves.why: metrics.json reports measured test_error_pct per cell (a_base 15.61%, a_strided 19.49%) with the full 12-model no-aug CIFAR-10 grid plus separate aug cells (c_allcnn_cifar10_aug, c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) in outputs/, matching the Tables 3/4 format except for the missing CIFAR-100 n

leaves.label: ILSVRC-2012 performance is measured as Top-1 validation error evaluated only on the center 224x224 patch of each validation image, matching
leaves.why: No measured ImageNet Top-1 error or center-224×224-patch evaluation protocol is present anywhere in the code or metrics.

leaves.label: Class probabilities at evaluation are produced by the paper's fully convolutional inference path (Section 2): the topmost 1-by-1 convolution
leaves.why: models.py AllCNNModel.forward produces per-position class scores via the final nn.Conv2d(192, num_classes, 1) and averages over all spatial positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before softmax/CE, with no nn.Linear anywhere in the model — the paper's fully-convolutional inference path, u

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduced base model A achieves approximately 12.5% test error on CIFAR-10 without data augmentation, matching the result reported in Secti
leaves.why: metrics.json (per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug.base) shows test_error_pct=15.61 after a full 350-epoch run (epochs_run=350, best_lr=0.05) — the model trains to the paper's regime and contradicts no directional claim, but the absolute number exceeds the leaf's ±1% band.

leaves.label: The reproduced metrics reproduce the qualitative ordering of Table 3 (Section 3.2): Strided-CNN-C performs worse than base model C, while Al
leaves.why: All four C cells ran (outputs/prj_.../c_{base,strided,convpool,allcnn}_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json exist) and the analogous A-family ordering holds in the visible metrics (a_strided 19.49% worse than a_base 15.61%), but the C-model numbers themselves — the core of this leaf — are

leaves.label: Reproduced All-CNN-C test error on CIFAR-10 (with and/or without augmentation) is at or below the corresponding base-model-C error, reproduc
leaves.why: The comparison cells exist and completed (outputs/prj_.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/, c_allcnn_cifar10_aug/, c_base_cifar10_noaug/ each with metrics.json and model.pt), but the visible metrics.json truncates inside a_strided before any C-model values, leaving the central claim unver

leaves.label: The reproduced ImageNet All-CNN-B achieves a Top-1 validation error close to 41.2% on the ILSVRC-2012 center 224x224 evaluation, with a mode
leaves.why: Only the architecture class exists (models.py build_imagenet_all_cnn_b); there is no measured Top-1 error anywhere in the evidence, so this result-match leaf is absent.

leaves.label: The repository contains runnable source code and configs for every evaluated variant — base models A/B/C, Strided-CNN-A/B/C, ConvPool-CNN-A/
leaves.why: models.py implements all 12 variants (build_all_cnn for letters A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn with Dropout(0.2) input and Dropout(0.5) per block), all 12 cifar10_noaug cells ran per the outputs/ listing, and the a_base train_loss_history in metrics.json shows discrete drop

leaves.label: Per-run training logs and a machine-readable metrics file (e.g. metrics.json) record the final test error for each model variant on CIFAR-10
leaves.why: Every variant cell has a per-run log and machine-readable metrics (outputs/prj_.../<cell>.log plus <cell>/metrics.json and training_curves.json for all 12 cifar10_noaug cells and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug / c_allcnn_cifar10_aug), and the visible metrics records traceability fields pe

leaves.label: Saved visualization artifacts exist for the guided-backpropagation method of Section 4: reconstructed feature visualizations for lower layer
leaves.why: Saved artifacts exist from the trained All-CNN-C (outputs/prj_.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/fig_relu_masking.png and guided_backprop.json), produced by guided_backprop.py which correctly implements the Section-4 rule (grad * (input>0) * (grad>0) in GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook),

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json (per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug.base) shows test_error_pct=15.61 after a full 350-epoch run (epochs_run=350, best_lr=0.05) — the model trains to the paper's regime and contradicts no directional claim, but the absolute number exceeds the leaf's ±1% band.

All four C cells ran (outputs/prj_.../c_{base,strided,convpool,allcnn}_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json exist) and the analogous A-family ordering holds in the visible metrics (a_strided 19.49% worse than a_base 15.61%), but the C-model numbers themselves — the core of this leaf — are not visible in the measured evidence.

The comparison cells exist and completed (outputs/prj_.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/, c_allcnn_cifar10_aug/, c_base_cifar10_noaug/ each with metrics.json and model.pt), but the visible metrics.json truncates inside a_strided before any C-model values, leaving the central claim unverified.
Only the architecture class exists (models.py build_imagenet_all_cnn_b); there is no measured Top-1 error anywhere in the evidence, so this result-match leaf is absent.

models.py implements all 12 variants (build_all_cnn for letters A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn with Dropout(0.2) input and Dropout(0.5) per block), all 12 cifar10_noaug cells ran per the outputs/ listing, and the a_base train_loss_history in metrics.json shows discrete drops at epochs ~200 and ~250 over a 350-epoch run, consistent with the [200,250,300] schedule — but the momentum/weight-decay values are not visible in any shown config.

Every variant cell has a per-run log and machine-readable metrics (outputs/prj_.../<cell>.log plus <cell>/metrics.json and training_curves.json for all 12 cifar10_noaug cells and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug / c_allcnn_cifar10_aug), and the visible metrics records traceability fields per cell (cell_id, dataset, augment, lr, best_lr=0.05, test_error_pct) as shown for a_base and a_strided.

Saved artifacts exist from the trained All-CNN-C (outputs/prj_.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/fig_relu_masking.png and guided_backprop.json), produced by guided_backprop.py which correctly implements the Section-4 rule (grad * (input>0) * (grad>0) in GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook), but no per-layer (conv1–3 / conv6/conv9) feature visualizations are generated.

models.py build_all_cnn (letter 'C', variant 'allcnn') replaces each maxpool with _conv_relu(96,96,3,stride=2,padding=1)/_conv_relu(192,192,3,stride=2,padding=1) (channels preserved) and ends with conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1) followed by GAP in AllCNNModel.forward (x.mean(dim=[2,3])), all-conv+ReLU throughout.

models.py implements letter A (single 5×5 conv per block + maxpool), letter B (5×5 conv followed by a 1×1 NiN conv per block + maxpool), and letter C (two 3×3 convs per block + maxpool), each topped by the conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1)+GAP head, matching Table 1; output dirs a_base/b_base/c_base confirm all three trained.

models.py variant 'strided' for letter C sets stride=2 on the conv immediately preceding each removed pool (_conv_relu(96,96,3,stride=2,padding=1) and _conv_relu(192,192,3,stride=2,padding=1)) and omits _maxpool(); the c_strided_cifar10_noaug output cell shows it was trained.

models.py variant 'convpool' inserts an extra dense 3×3 conv (_conv_relu(96,96,3,1,1) / _conv_relu(192,192,3,1,1) — same kernel size as the 3×3 pool) before each retained _maxpool(); output cells a/b/c_convpool_cifar10_noaug confirm execution.

guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes guided_grad = grad * (input>0).float() * (grad>0).float(), exactly the combined deconvnet+backprop ReLU mask with no pooling switches, and outputs/c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/guided_backprop.json + fig_relu_masking.png show it ran on the pool-free All-CNN-C.

data.py applies per-sample GCN (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening (compute_zca_matrix, epsilon=0.1, fit on train set) per Goodfellow et al., and cached matrices data/zca_cache/zca_cifar10_eps0.1.npz and zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz confirm it was applied to both datasets.
data.py _augment implements only hflip + random translation with pad=5 (max ±5px) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 up to the named online-flip/reflect-pad detail.

data.py load_cifar processes cifar100 through the identical GCN+ZCA pipeline (zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz cached), models.py instantiates the same AllCNNModel('C','allcnn') with num_classes=100, and only a single c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists (no per-dataset sweep dirs), but its lr/schedule values are not in the visible evidence.

metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 and the measured train_loss_history exhibits the schedule's discrete drops (~0.67→0.558 at epoch ~200, ~0.52→0.498 at ~250, slight drop near 300, mirrored by test_acc_history jumping 0.79→0.834 after epoch 200), confirming the 350-epoch S=[200,250,300] schedule, but the momentum value itself is unverified.

metrics.json carries lr/best_lr fields (best_lr=0.05 for a_base and a_strided) indicating a best-γ concept exists, but no measured results for the other three candidate rates appear in any visible artifact, so the searched-set requirement is only partially evidenced.

models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every letter/variant, matching Section 3.2; only the weight-decay value is unverified.
models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB concretely implements the 12-conv-layer Table 6 architecture (3+3+3+3 conv layers, fully-convolutional head), but the required training run is entirely absent.

metrics.json reports measured test_error_pct per cell (a_base 15.61%, a_strided 19.49%) with the full 12-model no-aug CIFAR-10 grid plus separate aug cells (c_allcnn_cifar10_aug, c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) in outputs/, matching the Tables 3/4 format except for the missing CIFAR-100 no-aug setting.
No measured ImageNet Top-1 error or center-224×224-patch evaluation protocol is present anywhere in the code or metrics.

models.py AllCNNModel.forward produces per-position class scores via the final nn.Conv2d(192, num_classes, 1) and averages over all spatial positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before softmax/CE, with no nn.Linear anywhere in the model — the paper's fully-convolutional inference path, used for the measured test_accuracy in metrics.json.

weak_leaves.justification: Only the architecture class exists (models.py build_imagenet_all_cnn_b); there is no measured Top-1 error anywhere in the evidence, so this result-match leaf is absent.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: No measured ImageNet Top-1 error or center-224×224-patch evaluation protocol is present anywhere in the code or metrics.

All four C cells ran (outputs/prj_.../c_{base,strided,convpool,allcnn}_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json exist) and the analogous A-family ordering holds in the visible metrics (a_strided 19.49% worse than a_base 15.61%), but the C-model numbers themselves — the core of this leaf — are not visible in the measured evidence.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: The comparison cells exist and completed (outputs/prj_.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/, c_allcnn_cifar10_aug/, c_base_cifar10_noaug/ each with metrics.json and model.pt), but the visible metrics.json truncates inside a_strided before any C-model values, leaving the central claim unverified.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: metrics.json carries lr/best_lr fields (best_lr=0.05 for a_base and a_strided) indicating a best-γ concept exists, but no measured results for the other three candidate rates appear in any visible artifact, so the searched-set requirement is only partially evidenced.
models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB concretely implements the 12-conv-layer Table 6 architecture (3+3+3+3 conv layers, fully-convolutional head), but the required training run is entirely absent.

metrics.json (per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug.base) shows test_error_pct=15.61 after a full 350-epoch run (epochs_run=350, best_lr=0.05) — the model trains to the paper's regime and contradicts no directional claim, but the absolute number exceeds the leaf's ±1% band.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: models.py implements all 12 variants (build_all_cnn for letters A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn with Dropout(0.2) input and Dropout(0.5) per block), all 12 cifar10_noaug cells ran per the outputs/ listing, and the a_base train_loss_history in metrics.json shows discrete drops at epochs ~200 and ~250 over a 350-epoch run, consistent with the [200,250,300] schedule — but the momentum/weight-decay values are not visible in any shown config.
